problem.name: SASRec_Pet_Recommendation
problem.description:
You are an expert ML engineer tasked with improving a SASRec (Self-Attentive Sequential Recommendation) model on the Pet domain. The model uses multi-head self-attention with causal masking and sampled softmax loss.

Hardware / Runtime Requirements:
- CPU or GPU. Use device handling (e.g., device = torch.device('cuda'); model.to(device)).
- Training time is limited to ~30 minutes per epoch. Keep model changes efficient.

Libraries Allowed:
- numpy, scipy, pandas, torch, scikit-learn, networkx, transformers, sentence-transformers, regex, RapidFuzz, matplotlib, shapely, omegaconf, PyYAML, openai, requests, tqdm, safetensors

Model Architecture:
- SASRec uses multi-head self-attention with causal masking, point-wise feed-forward networks, and sampled softmax loss.
- Key hyperparameters: hidden_dim=128, num_blocks=1, num_heads=1, dropout=0.2.

Acceptance Checklist:
- Training loss decreases and stays finite.
- HR@5 and NDCG@5 metrics improve over the baseline.

重要约束：纯序列化建模，仅使用用户交互序列（item IDs），不可以加入文本嵌入（text embedding）、图像特征、评论内容等任何外部模态信息。只允许使用序列中的 item ID 进行建模。

initial_idea.title: SASRec: Self-Attentive Sequential Recommendation for Pet
initial_idea.content: SASRec uses multi-head self-attention with causal masking to model user interaction sequences. Unlike RNN-based models, self-attention captures long-range dependencies with adaptive weights. The model uses sampled softmax loss for efficient training on large item catalogs. Applied to the Amazon Pet dataset with leave-one-out evaluation.
